name: Balsa de Cardete, Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense y la antigua estación — circular desde Murchante
description: Jog this 9-mile (14.5 km) route from Murchante, exploring Balsa de Cardete, the Old Station, and views towards Ermita de San Gregorio Ostien
region_intro: Jogging routes around Murchante traverse a landscape characterized by agricultural fields and gentle, rolling terrain. The region features several water bodies, such as the Balsa de Pulguer and Balsa de Cardete, offering scenic paths. Routes often incorporate historical points like the Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense and the old Murchante station. The low elevation gains across the area make it suitable for various running abilities. Best jogging routes around Murchante The most popular jogging route is Murchante East Loop, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes 55 minutes to complete. This circular route offers a straightforward path around the eastern side of the town. Another top favourite among local runners is Balsa de Pulguer — circular, a moderate 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This route provides pleasant running around a local reservoir, ideal for a shorter outing. Local runners also love the Balsa de Cardete, Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense y la antigua estación — circular desde Murchante, a 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and past historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Jogging around Murchante is defined by agricultural paths, local reservoirs, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more moderate running challenges. The routes in Murchante are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Murchante's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Murchante?
A: There are over 90 running routes in the Murchante area, offering a wide variety of options for different abilities and preferences. These include 7 easy routes, 76 moderate routes, and 11 more challenging trails.

Q: Are there easy running trails suitable for beginners in Murchante?
A: Yes, Murchante offers several easy running trails perfect for beginners. The region is characterized by low elevation gains, making many paths accessible. A good option for a gentle run is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145035877/balsa-de-pulguer-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Pulguer — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 3.2-mile (5.2 km) path around a local reservoir.

Q: What are some longer running routes for experienced runners in Murchante?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, Murchante has 11 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147849134/balsa-de-cardete-ermita-de-san-gregorio-ostiens-y-la-antigua-estacion-circular-desde-murchante" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Cardete, Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense y la antigua estación — circular desde Murchante&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.0-mile (14.5 km) trail that takes you through agricultural landscapes and past historical sites.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Murchante area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Murchante are circular. Popular choices include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146354456/murchante-east-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murchante East Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.9-mile (7.9 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145035877/balsa-de-pulguer-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Pulguer — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a pleasant 3.2-mile (5.2 km) loop.

Q: Are the running trails in Murchante suitable for families?
A: Many of the easier and shorter routes in Murchante are suitable for families, especially those with older children. The low elevation gains and well-defined paths make for a pleasant experience. Consider routes around the local reservoirs like Balsa de Pulguer for a family-friendly outing.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails around Murchante?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most outdoor trails in the Murchante area, especially on agricultural paths and rural tracks. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near agricultural fields or livestock, and to check local regulations for specific areas or nature reserves.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running routes in Murchante?
A: The running routes in Murchante offer a mix of agricultural landscapes and natural beauty. You'll often find paths traversing fields, with scenic views around water bodies like the Balsa de Pulguer and Balsa de Cardete. Some routes also pass by historical points such as the Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense and the old Murchante station.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Murchante?
A: The best times of year for running in Murchante are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for ideal conditions. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the running routes?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical points and natural features. You might pass by the Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense or the old Murchante station. For more significant landmarks nearby, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4766416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Santa María de Tudela&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5784182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de los Fueros de Tudela&lt;/a&gt;, which are a short distance from Murchante.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Murchante?
A: The running routes in Murchante are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the peaceful agricultural paths, and the scenic views around the local reservoirs, making it a rewarding experience for all abilities.

Q: Are there any running routes that start from nearby towns like Tudela or Cascante?
A: Yes, you can find running routes that connect to Murchante from neighboring towns. For instance, there's a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Tudela&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Murchante Station loop from Cascante&lt;/a&gt;, offering extended options for exploring the wider region.
tips: The line was inaugurated in 1892 and functioned for many years as an important means of transportation for the region. It stopped providing railway services and was closed in the 1960s. Very close to the station you can enjoy a rest and recreation area on the Vía Verde del Tarazonica.
Jesús Basiano was a Spanish landscape painter. His work is characterized by its own varied style, ranging from landscapes and still life to portraits and abstract works.
Murchante is located in the Ribera de Navarra region, an area characterized by its natural beauty and agricultural landscapes.

faqs.question: Where does the Balsa de Cardete, Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense y la antigua estación — circular desde Murchante trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: This circular route typically starts and ends near the Old Murchante Station. There is likely parking available in the vicinity of the old station, which now serves as a rest and recreation area along the Vía Verde del Tarazonica.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as "moderate" and requires "Good fitness" due to its 14.5 km distance. However, it is also described as "Suitable for all skill levels" because of its mostly paved surfaces. If you're a beginner, be prepared for the distance, but the terrain itself is not technically challenging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is characterized by mostly paved surfaces, making it relatively smooth and comfortable for jogging. You'll encounter sections of the Vía Verde del Tarazonica, which is a rehabilitated old railway line, ensuring a well-maintained path.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Balsa de Cardete, Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense y la antigua estación — circular desde Murchante route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience varied scenery, including the tranquil natural area of Balsa de Cardete with its abundant water plants. The route also passes the historic Old Murchante Station, part of the Vía Verde del Tarazonica, and offers views related to the Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense. You might also spot the Bust of Basiano and the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Murchante.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail uses public paths like the Vía Verde del Tarazonica, specific regulations for dogs are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas like Balsa de Cardete, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mostly paved surfaces and natural areas, spring and autumn are generally ideal for jogging this route. The weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery, especially around Balsa de Cardete, would be vibrant. Summer might be hot, and winter could be chilly, but the paved nature makes it accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access this jogging route. It utilizes public paths, including sections of the Vía Verde del Tarazonica, which are freely accessible for recreational use.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails, most notably the Vía Verde del Tarazonica. You will also briefly cross parts of the Camino Natural del Agua Soriano, Camino Antonino, and Camino de la Vera Cruz (El camino de los Templarios).

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see around Balsa de Cardete?
faqs.answer: Balsa de Cardete is a natural area teeming with flora and fauna. You can expect to see abundant water plants like reeds, tamarisks, and canes. While specific wildlife sightings aren't guaranteed, such wetland environments often host various bird species, amphibians, and insects.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trail in Murchante?
faqs.answer: The Old Murchante Station, which is part of the route, serves as a rest and recreation area. Murchante itself is a town, so you can expect to find cafes, shops, and other amenities there before or after your jog.

faqs.question: Is the Ermita de San Gregorio Ostiense mentioned in the trail name the famous basilica in Sorlada?
faqs.answer: No, the trail name likely refers to a local hermitage or offers distant views towards a notable religious site. The famous Basílica de San Gregorio Ostiense in Sorlada is a considerable distance from Murchante and is not directly part of this specific jogging loop.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Murchante
